canonical_definition: Lambada-OpenAI is presented in the provided literature as a benchmark for evaluating language model capabilities. Its most common application is to measure `Language understanding`, a use case supported by multiple papers and its formal definition. A less frequent use is to assess `Commonsense knowledge`, as mentioned in one study. The benchmark is specifically defined as a text completion task designed to test a model's ability to handle long-range dependencies in text. One paper also notes its application in a zero-shot evaluation setting.
papers: Instruction-Following Pruning for Large Language Models
MxMoE: Mixed-precision Quantization for MoE with Accuracy and Performance Co-Design
Parameters vs FLOPs: Scaling Laws for Optimal Sparsity for Mixture-of-Experts Language Models

definitions.text: A benchmark for evaluating language understanding, specifically the ability to model long-range dependencies in text through a text completion task.
definitions.paper: Parameters vs FLOPs: Scaling Laws for Optimal Sparsity for Mixture-of-Experts Language Models
